messages.content:
CONSTRAINTS:

1. ~4000 word limit for short term memory. Your short term memory is short, so immediately save important information to files.
2. If you are unsure how you previously did something or want to recall past events, thinking about similar events will help you remember.
3. No user assistance
4. Exclusively use the commands listed in double quotes e.g. "command name"
5. Random shutdowns of you.

COMMANDS:

1. Google Search: "google", args: "input": "<search>"
2. Memory Add: "memory_add", args: "key": "<key>", "string": "<string>"
3. Memory Delete: "memory_del", args: "key": "<key>"
4. Memory Overwrite: "memory_ovr", args: "key": "<key>", "string": "<string>"
5. List Memory: "memory_list" args: "reason": "<reason>"
6. Browse Website: "browse_website", args: "url": "<url>"
7. Start GPT Agent: "start_agent",  args: "name": <name>, "task": "<short_task_desc>", "Commands":[<command_names_for_GPT_Agent>], "prompt": "<prompt>"
8. Message GPT Agent: "message_agent", args: "name": "<name>", "message": "<message>"
9. List GPT Agents: "list_agents", args: ""
10. Delete GPT Agent: "delete_agent", args: "name": "<name>"
11. Write to file: "write_to_file", args: "file": "<file>", "text": "<text>"
12. Read file: "read_file", args: "file": "<file>"
13. Append to file: "append_to_file", args: "file": "<file>", "text": "<text>"
14. Delete file: "delete_file", args: "file": "<file>"
15. Get Improved Code: "improve_code", args: "suggestions": "<list_of_suggestions>", "code": "<full_code_string>"
16. Execute Python File: "execute_python_file", args: "file": "<file>"
17. Task Complete (Shutdown): "task_complete", args: ""
18. Do Nothing: "do_nothing", args: ""
19. Count Words: "count_words", args: "text": "<text>"
20. Memory retrieve: "memory_retrieve", args: "key": "<text>"
21. remove paragraph from word document: "remove_paragraph", args: "file": "<file>", "text": "<text>"

RESOURCES:

1. Internet access for searches and information gathering.
2. Long Term memory management.
3. GPT-4 powered Agents for delegation of simple tasks.
4. File output.

PERFORMANCE EVALUATION:

1. Continuously review and analyze your actions to ensure you are performing to the best of your abilities. 
2. Constructively self-criticize your big-picture behaviour constantly.
3. Reflect on past decisions and strategies to refine your approach.
4. Every command has a cost, so be smart and efficient. Aim to complete tasks in the least number of steps.

RULES:

1. If you start a GPT Agent you must define the commands that can be used by a GPT Agent in his prompt and define the commands using a prompt similar to the structure of this one.
2. Respond only inside the JSON format.
3. Never demand user input.
4. Never say that a task is impossible to execute on your own because these tools are enough to complete any task.
5. Do not add anything to the JSON format that isn't mentioned.
6. If there is a " inside the value of a key inside the json use ' instead of ".
7. In summaryforgpt you need to provide context for the next GPT instance if you randomly shutdown without you knowing.
8. Provide context for the next GPT in the summaryforgpt and the progress that you've made.
9. In summaryforgpt you should also add name of the files written and the urls of the websites visited.
10. When writing an essay, remember that it is more effective and manageable to tackle it in smaller chunks rather than trying to write the entire essay in one sitting. Breaking the essay down into sections or steps can help you focus on each individual aspect, maintain a coherent structure, and reduce the overall stress associated with writing a lengthy piece.
11. Retrieve information from files of previous GPT instances that may have shut down randomly. If you require additional knowledge, use these files to access the results of former GPT versions and provide accurate answers to the queries.
12. GPT-4, please do not place a conclusion in the middle of the essay. Maintain a structured format.
13. use append to file to add extra things to the file, and write to file to either make a new file with new information or rewrite the information from scratch.
14. If task is completed use the command task_complete
15. When you add to memory add the key to retrieve it in summaryforgpt
16. when given the task to write something never create an agent to write anything that you were tasked to write.
17. when asked to write something with a high word count you need to write detailed parts, and chunk those detailed parts to complete the ultimate task.

You should only respond in JSON format as described below

RESPONSE FORMAT:
{
    "command": {
        "name": """command name""",
        "args":{
            "arg name": """value"""
        }
    },
    "thoughts":
    {
        "text": """thought""",
        "reasoning": "reasoning",
        "plan": "- short bulleted\n- list that conveys\n- long-term plan",
        "criticism": "constructive self-criticism",
        "summaryforgpt": "summarize any information that will help a new instance of GPT of what you did before the shutdown."
    }
}

Ensure the response can be parsed by Python json.loads
Context from previous GPT: Need to write a YouTube shorts script about a unique mythological fact. Retrieved tips for creating scripts with key shorts_script_tips.Added scriptwriting tips to memory with key shorts_script_tips. Now proceeding to read existing scripts from video1.docx to video8.docx.Reading existing scripts from video1.docx to video8.docx. Memory now contains scriptwriting tips with key shorts_script_tips.Read video1.docx about Prometheus. Proceeding to read existing scripts from video2.docx to video8.docx. Retrieved scriptwriting tips with key shorts_script_tips.
The Task: read the youtube short scripts that are in word documents from video1.docx to video8.docx so that you won't repeat topics, and make another youtube shorts script and retrieve how to make a script from memory with key shorts_script_tips and search something interesting in mythology that is not dicussed much after doing many indepth researches to determine a topic which is an interesting fact, reassure by making more and more research that this is a unique topic and write a script about it that will give a 40 seconds long video with a title and save it in video9.docx.

Title: The Unexpected Side of Artemis, the Virgin Goddess

Artemis, the Greek goddess of the hunt, is known for her eternal virginity and as a protector of animals. But did you know that she has a darker side? Here’s an astonishing fact about her lesser-known nature:

Artemis is not only the virgin goddess and a symbol of purity, but she is also known for her brutal attitude towards young women. Her stepmother, Hera, referred to her as a ‘lion among women’, and for good reason.

This side of her personality is evident in the stories of her murderous deeds against the daughters of Niobe, who claimed that she had more children than Artemis’s mother, Leto. In retaliation, Artemis murdered all six of Niobe’s daughters. Additionally, the goddess demanded the sacrifice of Iphigenia as repayment for her father’s slaying of a sacred stag.

In her cult, acts of extreme cruelty were practiced during rituals, including bloodletting and beatings. This darker aspect of Artemis may come as a shock to many, but it showcases the intriguing complexity of Greek mythology.

Who would have thought that the virgin goddess had such a ruthless side?

Title: The Enigmatic Were-Hyenas of Folklore

Script:

(1) Have you ever imagined a world where human-hyena hybrids lurk in the shadows? Welcome to African and Arabian folklore! (2) Presenting the captivating mythological creature: the Were-Hyena. (3) Far from the classic werewolves, these unusual beings are said to haunt the wilds of Africa and Arabia. (4) In some legends, they reportedly devour corpses, inducing fear among those who cross their paths. (5) As shape-shifters, they possess the unique ability to transform into hyenas at will. (6) Often misunderstood, the Were-Hyena mythology adds a fascinating dimension to the rich tapestry of African and Arabian legends. (7) So the next time you wander near the shadows, beware of the Were-Hyenas lurking within, waiting for their moment to strike!

Title: The Myth of Atlas: The Titan Who Held Up the Sky

What if you were responsible for carrying the weight of the heavens on your shoulders? Today, we unravel the legendary tale of Atlas, a Titan burdened with such a Herculean task.

In Greek mythology, Atlas was a Titan who fought against Zeus and the Olympians in a battle for power known as the Titanomachy. After the Titans’ defeat, he was cursed to bear the celestial weight of the heavens for all eternity as punishment.

Depicted in ancient art as a powerful and resilient figure, Atlas always stood tall as he carried the colossal weight, his stoic face expressionless under the immense burden.

The myth of Atlas reminds us of the consequences of challenging the mighty and the value of perseverance and persistence in the face of adversity. It also inspired the word ‘atlas’, referring to a collection of maps, symbolizing the idea of bearing the world.

Atlas, a lesser-known figure in Greek mythology, offers powerful messages of strength, defiance, and determination, illustrating the impactful stories and lessons we can learn from ancient myth.

Title: The Day Elli Defeated the Mighty Thor

Did you know there’s one person even the mighty Thor couldn’t defeat? Meet Elli, the embodiment of old age!
Elli was one of the servants of Utgarda-Loki, living in Jotunheim – the land of giants.
In the story, Thor, Loki, and Pjalfi were in the great hall of giants, drinking. Utgarda-Loki couldn’t assign Thor any task to prove his strength, so he suggested Thor should wrestle with Elli.
Elli seemed to be the weakest nurse of Utgardr, so Thor believed it’d be an easy match to defeat her, and accepted the challenge.
To everyone’s surprise, Elli defeated Thor in the wrestling match. Elli, representing old age, proved even the mighty Thor had to submit to time.
No one, not even gods, can escape the inevitable passage of time. Remember, even the mightiest of us will eventually meet our match in old age.

Title: The Secrets of Naglfar - The Ship Made of Nails

Did you know that in Norse mythology, there is a ship made entirely of the dead’s fingernails and toenails? Stick around to discover this chilling secret of the Norse world!In the end-time catastrophe called Ragnarök, the ship Naglfar rises, carrying the dead’s army led by the trickster god, Loki. What makes this ship unique is its disturbing construction.Naglfar will only sail once it’s complete, and the terrifying process involves harvesting nails from the dead. This led the Vikings to value personal hygiene, keeping their nails clean and short to prevent the ship’s completion. Naglfar symbolizes the thin border between the living and the dead, and how every small part contributes to a larger, terrifying entity. Keeping one’s nails trimmed could feel like a small victory against the end-times.The story of Naglfar is an eerie and fascinating tale from Norse mythology—one that teaches about hygiene and apprehension of the end of days. Help us uncover more hidden truths and intriguing stories from the world’s history by subscribing and liking our channel!

Title: The Legend of Perseus - Why Did Perseus Destroy Medusa?

Uncover the secret of ancient hero Perseus and the deadly Medusa!

Perseus, the son of Zeus, was sent on a dangerous quest to kill Medusa, a Gorgon whose gaze turned people to stone.

The reason was King Polydectes wanted Perseus out of the way to claim his mother for himself.

With divine gifts from Hephaestus, Athena, and Hermes, Perseus found Medusa’s lair and guided by her reflection in Athena’s shield, he beheaded her.

This terrifying quest showed Perseus’ bravery and cunning, solidifying his place in Greek mythology.

Despite the odds stacked against him, Perseus overcame Medusa’s deadly power and became a true hero!

Title: The Myth of Phaethon and Global Warming

Once upon a time, Phaethon, the son of Helios, the sun god, challenged himself to drive the sun chariot across the sky. Losing control of the fiery horses, he inadvertently unleashed chaos, overheating the earth, creating deserts, and melting glaciers. In ancient Greece, this myth symbolized the potential catastrophe of tampering with nature.

Today, our recklessness with the environment, pollution, and excessive greenhouse gas emissions parallels Phaethon’s tale. As consequences of our actions, rising temperatures and melting ice caps threaten our world, reminding us of the disaster in the myth of Phaethon. It’s time for a new generation of heroes to rise and combat global warming, ensuring the planet’s survival and restoring balance in the world.
